metric_value: Eight enterprise application categories mapped to storage access characteristics
notes: Foundational taxonomy for midline storage category creation project

metric_value: Update intensive — both reads and writes with focus on writes
notes: Example: order entry systems; highest I/O intensity; needs high-performance storage

metric_value: Mix of writes and reads — updating for transactions plus random reads
notes: Example: SAP R/3; distinguishes from traditional OLTP by read component
Example: data warehousing; low write intensity; suited to lower-cost sequential storage

metric_value: Primarily random reads with a little transaction processing
notes: File/database hybrid; moderate I/O; candidate for midline storage

metric_value: Write once; read once to many times
notes: Low active I/O; high retention value; key midline storage target

metric_value: Mix of reads and writes on large pieces of data
notes: High data volume; moderate I/O frequency; e.g. CAD/CAM and post-production editing

metric_value: After creation content is fixed — ideally inalterable; read-only
notes: Example: X-rays and MRIs; immutability desired; candidate for WORM/nearline storage

metric_value: Read-only access of previously-created large bit-mapped files
notes: Example: MP3 and video files; high throughput sequential reads; no write I/O after creation

metric_value: Application I/O analysis supports distinct midline storage tier between FC and desktop ATA
notes: Framework implies ~5 of 8 application categories are candidates for midline rather than premium storage
Deferred: verify whether tiered/nearline storage became mainstream category distinct from high-performance and archive

metric_value: Different application access patterns justify different storage tiers with distinct cost-performance profiles
notes: This classification is foundational analytical support for ILM policies

metric_value: Sequential-read BI queries are over-provisioned on high-performance random-I/O SCSI storage
notes: Implies cost savings opportunity by migrating BI to midline sequential storage

metric_value: Write-once read-few email pattern creates accumulating storage demand suited to lower-cost nearline
notes: Implies email archiving as a key nearline/midline growth market

metric_value: Web apps primarily read-biased which reduces need for high-write-performance premium storage
notes: Justifies placing web serving content on midline storage rather than FC drives
